---
title: Randomness in Reinforcement Learning
description: 'The two sources of stochasticity in RL: the policy and the environment.'
categories:
- Foundations
---

In RL, randomness comes from two places:

- **Policy** — $a_{t} \sim \pi(\cdot \mid s_{t})$: the agent samples an action from its policy distribution. For example, in state $s$ the policy might say:
  - Left: 0.7
  - Right: 0.3
- **Environment** — $s_{t+1} \sim p(\cdot \mid s_{t}, a_{t})$: the world samples the next state given the current state and action. For example, if the robot tries to move right:
  - 80%: it goes right
  - 20%: it slips and stays still
